## How It's Calculated

Evaluates LCP/FID/CLS metrics and average · Free and fast.

This calculator applies the formula with your input values, checking typical ranges and alerting when values seem unreasonable.

## Final Notes

This calculation is a reference guide only. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult a specialized professional. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.q: What is LCP and what's a good score?
faq.a: LCP (Largest Contentful Paint) measures loading performance. A good score is under 2.5 seconds.
FID measures how quickly the browser responds to user input. A good score is under 100ms. Note: INP replaced FID in 2024 with a good threshold of under 200ms.

faq.q: What does CLS mean in web performance?
faq.a: CLS (Cumulative Layout Shift) measures unexpected layout changes during page load. A good score is under 0.1.
Core Web Vitals are Google's metrics measuring real-world page experience: loading speed (LCP), interactivity (FID/INP), and visual stability (CLS).

faq.q: How can I improve Core Web Vitals?
faq.a: Image optimization, resource preloading, code splitting, lazy loading, minimizing JavaScript, and reducing render-blocking resources.

faq.q: Is this calculator free to use?
faq.a: Yes, all our calculators are completely free with no registration required.
Results are for reference. We use internationally validated formulas. For critical decisions, consult a professional.
No. All calculations happen in your browser. We never send data to any server.
